cards.content:
# Shape 1: "This is who I am"

The display piece. Portfolio, personal brand, practice presence.

Architecture firms, therapists, lawyers, consultants. The website *represents* them. Their work happens elsewhere.

The original wish-fulfilling jewel.

Standard shape: about + services + credentials + contact.

# Shape 2: "This thing is happening"

The event coordinator. Wedding, conference, gala, Airbnb stay, retreat.

The website isn't the main thing — it coordinates around the thing. RSVP, FAQ, logistics, communications.

The organizer is focused on the EVENT, not the site. Time-sensitive. Repeatable structure.

# Shape 3: "This thing exists. Come look."

The advertisement / communication piece.

House listing, book launch, fundraiser, product launch.

Pure: here is a thing, here's why you should care, here's what to do next.

Often time-sensitive. Speed is the entire value.

# The Big Play: Local Practice Disruption

Every local practitioner (dentist, therapist, vet, lawyer, med spa) has a website that's a piece of shit. The entire agency ecosystem is a racket.

What if someone who actually gives a shit showed up?

- Only work with high-rated businesses (4.5+ Google)
- Proactive outreach — rebuild their site and SHOW them
- Measure conversions
- Full stack cheaper than the current racket

The play: scrape reviews, rebuild, show side-by-side. "This is what you have. This is what you could have."

# Wedding Complete System

The deep play. Everything matching. Everything coherent.

- Stationery + website + RSVP
- AI guest chat ("where do I stay?")
- Food tracking + coordination
- Bride manages nothing

Nobody else does this. The matching is the killer feature.

~2M weddings/year in the US. $30K-$50K avg budget. Digital/stationery is a real line item.

# The Lifecycle

Baby Showers -> Bar Mitzvahs / Quinceaneras -> Weddings -> Funerals

All the same shape. All one-time. All emotionally charged. All have budget.

The company that holds your most important life moments.

Each is a "this thing is happening" shape with deep personal meaning.

# Professional Services

Architects, lawyers, financial advisors, consultants, accountants.

Fat margins. They pay for quality. Their hourly rate is $300-$1,000 — a $3K website is a few hours of their own billing.

Standard shape: about + services + credentials + contact.

One new client from the site pays for it many times over.

# Healthcare Practices

Dentists, therapists, med spas, vets, PT, fertility clinics, dietitians.

Huge volume. Most websites are terrible. Trust is everything on these sites.

Perfect target for the Big Play — proactive rebuild and show. AI intake/screening is an obvious add.

# Creative Professionals

Photographers, interior designers, videographers, tattoo artists, artists.

Portfolio-driven. They CARE about aesthetics but can't make their own site.

Network effects — designers know designers. Referrals happen because the site actually feels like the person.

Smart portfolio exploration is the AI differentiator here.

# Real Estate

Agent personal sites, per-property listing pages, development marketing sites, venue sites.

Recurring per agent. Speed is critical. 1.5M agents in the US.

$2B+ annual market. Luxury developers pay $10K+ for project-specific sites. One booking/sale from the site pays for everything.

# Events & Hospitality

Conferences, galas, retreats, boutique hotels, restaurants, Airbnb/vacation rentals.

Time-sensitive. Repeatable. The "this thing is happening" shape.

Marketing budgets are real. One booking from the site pays for it. The site needs to feel like *being there*.

# Solopreneurs / Freelancers

Coaches, speakers, authors, course creators, podcasters.

Natural network — they know each other, they recommend each other.

Lower ticket but massive volume and word-of-mouth. The ones with existing clients and revenue are the sweet spot.

# "The phone call IS the product"

The website is the artifact. The listening is the service.

AI made production near-free. The only remaining bottleneck is the translation — hearing what someone means and making it real.

# SEO Per Niche

Same play as wedding SEO. Build problem-specific pages for every niche.

Each page has content + tool + CTA.

Auto-generate hundreds of niche-specific examples as proof of concept. Free content that drives traffic to paid service.

# Proactive Rebuild & Show

Scrape their Google reviews. Look at their current site. Rebuild it. Show them side by side.

"This is what you have. This is what you could have."

No pitch deck needed — the proof is the product itself.

# AI Features as Differentiator

- Chat-against-your-site
- AI intake/screening
- Smart portfolio exploration
- "Ask about this property/venue/practice"

Nobody else does this. They're still thinking of websites as documents, not as active participants in what the visitor came to do.

# Only High-Rated Businesses

4.5+ on Google Maps. Businesses that actually care.

A website can't fix a bad business. Help great businesses become visible.

This is also a quality filter for your own sanity.

# The Client Delivery Portal (Future)

Not just display, but the layer between practitioner and client.

Intake, scheduling, communication, custom portals.

All the booking/intake sites suck. Become their "digital person" — the one who holds their world and makes things show up.

# Frankenstein Design

Copy elements from sites you love, combine them. Not wholesale copying — inspiration blending.

Fast way to get good design. Leverages taste as a skill.

# Auto Dealerships

Need to stand out. AI features are obvious to sell to them.

Worth exploring as a niche. High budgets, competitive market, receptive to tech differentiation.

# AI-First Publishing

Audiobook interface: TTS + STT + click to play + talk to AI about it.

Distribution is the key question. B2C and B2B distribution makes more sense than direct.

A different shape entirely — worth tracking but not the core play.

# Memorial / Funeral Sites

The other side of weddings. 2.8M deaths/year.

Extreme urgency (24-48hrs). Deep felt-sense listening. Super basic technically.

Low price but massive volume. Speed is everything. The emotional weight demands real care.

# Speed

Same day delivery. Agencies take weeks.

This alone is worth $2-5K. "I've been meaning to do this for two years. You did it in an hour."

For time-sensitive launches, speed IS the entire value.

# Full Concierge

Domain, hosting, design, copy, build, deploy. They touch nothing.

The anti-Squarespace. The anti-Lovable. They don't learn a tool, pick a template, or manage a dashboard.

It's done.

# The Listening Layer

Carrying forward whatever they need. Philosophy, features, problems, things they didn't know to ask for.

This is what makes it not-commodity. The reason the architect said "this is what I wanted" — when he couldn't have told you beforehand.

Every client gets this, scaled to what they bring.

# Context Compounds

Each engagement gets faster. The second thing takes 5 minutes, not 15.

Scope expands naturally per client: website -> proposals -> portal -> new division.

You become their "digital person." Not their web designer. The person who holds their world.
